recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Chicken Fajita Plates With Mild Lime Yoghurt Crema

Lime-led marinated chicken fajita plates with charred peppers, two warm Aldi protein wraps each, cheddar, coriander and a mild savoury yoghurt crema.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 45 minutes active, plus at least 1 hour marinating

Yield: 3 fajita plates, 6 wraps

Meal role: dinner

## Ingredients

### Meat

- Ashfields Chicken Drumstick Fillets - 355g, sliced into thin strips

### Pantry

- Village Bakery Tortilla Protein Wraps - 6 wraps
- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - 22.5ml, 1 1/2 tbsp
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - 5g, 2 1/2 tsp
- Stonemill Ground Cumin - 5g, 2 1/2 tsp
- Stonemill Dried Oregano - 1g, 1 tsp

### Fruit And Veg

- Mixed Peppers - 2 peppers, 320g, sliced from the normal 3-pack
- Brown Onion - 1 medium onion, 110g, sliced
- Garlic - 3 small cloves, 9g, finely grated or chopped
- Limes - 1 1/2, juiced
- Fresh Coriander - 11g, chopped

### Dairy

- Fat Free Greek Style Yoghurt - 165g
- Mature Cheddar - 70g, grated

## Equipment

- 10 inch cast iron frying pan
- Mixing bowl
- Small bowl
- Tongs
- Chopping board and knife
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Seasoning bowl: mix the lime juice, olive oil, garlic, paprika, cumin, oregano, salt and black pepper.
- Crema bowl: spoon a little clean seasoning into the yoghurt before raw chicken touches the marinade.
- Chicken bowl: slice the chicken into thin strips, removing tough tendon pieces.
- Veg board: slice the peppers and onion into thin strips.
- Wrap and finish station: set out the 6 wraps, grated cheddar and chopped coriander.

## Method

### 1. Prep ahead: marinate the chicken

Spoon a small amount of clean lime seasoning into the crema bowl first. Add the chicken to the remaining seasoning, mix well, cover and chill for at least 1 hour.

Uses:

- Chicken - 355g
- Lime, oil, garlic, paprika, cumin and oregano seasoning - most of the bowl

### 2. Make the mild crema

Stir the reserved clean lime seasoning through the yoghurt until it is tangy, mild and spoonable.

Uses:

- Fat free Greek style yoghurt - 165g

### 3. Char the chicken

Heat the cast iron pan until properly hot. Cook the chicken in two batches, leaving it alone at first so it chars instead of steaming. Lift the cooked chicken out to a plate.

Uses:

- Marinated chicken - all

### 4. Char the peppers and onion

Use the same hot pan for the peppers and onion. Cook until they pick up char but still have bite.

Uses:

- Sliced peppers and onion - all

### 5. Combine the fajita mix and warm the wraps

Return the chicken to the pan with the vegetables for 1-2 minutes. Warm the protein wraps one at a time in the dry pan.

Uses:

- Chicken and vegetables - all
- Protein wraps - 6

### 6. Plate

Serve this as fajita plates, not one overloaded wrap. Put the fajita mix on the plate, add the crema, cheddar and coriander, and serve the two warm wraps alongside each plate.

Uses:

- Mild lime yoghurt crema - all
- Cheddar - 70g
- Fresh coriander - 11g

## Plate

| Finished element | Luke | Sarah | Louis |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Finished fajita mix | about half, served with 2 wraps | about one third, served with 2 wraps | about one fifth, served with 2 wraps |
| Warm protein wraps | 2 wraps | 2 wraps | 2 wraps |
| Mild lime yoghurt crema | 75g | 60g | 30g |
| Cheddar and coriander finish | 35g cheddar, 5g coriander | 25g cheddar, 5g coriander | 10g cheddar, 1g coriander |

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 900.5 kcal, 73.7g protein, 76.7g carbs, 33.5g fat, GBP 2.76, 184.1 fullness
- Sarah: 743.8 kcal, 55.7g protein, 72.3g carbs, 25.7g fat, GBP 1.99, 144.2 fullness
- Louis: 551.3 kcal, 37.7g protein, 66.0g carbs, 14.9g fat, GBP 1.25, 109.1 fullness

## Cook's Notes

- The marinating wait is a prep-ahead task, not active cooking time.
- Do not crowd the pan. Two chicken batches are better than steamed chicken.
- Keep the heat high enough for char, then warm the wraps after the fajita mix is ready.
- The wraps are the carb for the plate. They are not expected to close around the whole dinner portion.
- The yoghurt crema is a mild savoury sauce, not a sweet yoghurt topping.
- Plate percentages are rounded for practical serving; the exact nutrition and cost still come from the saved person rows.

# Chicken Fajita Plates With Mild Lime Yoghurt Crema - Plate Portions

Source: saved `datasheet.json` finished serving elements, person rows, or component shares. The renderer does not invent serving splits.

| Component | Luke | Sarah | Louis | Basis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Finished fajita mix | about half of the finished fajita mix, served with 2 wraps | about one third of the finished fajita mix, served with 2 wraps | about one fifth of the finished fajita mix, served with 2 wraps | share_of_finished_batch |
| Warm protein wraps | 2 warm protein wraps | 2 warm protein wraps | 2 warm protein wraps | count_each |
| Mild lime yoghurt crema | 75g crema | 60g crema | 30g crema | direct_amount |
| Cheddar and coriander finish | 35g cheddar and 5g coriander | 25g cheddar and 5g coriander | 10g cheddar and 1g coriander | direct_amount |

# Chicken Fajita Plates With Mild Lime Yoghurt Crema - Recipe Shopping List

Visible shopping rows only. Neutral helpers such as water, salt, and black pepper are method-only and not listed for shopping.

## Main Buy

### Dairy

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - need 165g; pack 1kg; used cost GBP 0.24
- Emporium British Mature Cheddar Cheese - need 70g; pack 400g; used cost GBP 0.44

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick Brown Onions - need 1 medium onion; pack loose or multipack onions; used cost GBP 0.11
- Nature's Pick Garlic - need 3 small cloves; pack 4 bulb pack; used cost GBP 0.39
- Nature's Pick Limes 5 Pack - need 1.5 limes; pack 5 lime pack; used cost GBP 0.26
- Nature's Pick Mixed Peppers - need 2 peppers; pack 3 pepper pack; used cost GBP 1.19
- Ready, Set... Cook! British Cut Coriander - need 11g; pack 30g; used cost GBP 0.18

### Meat

- Ashfields Chicken Drumstick Fillets - need 355g; pack 600g; used cost GBP 1.76

### Pantry

- Solesta Extra Virgin Olive Oil - need 1 1/2 tbsp (22.5ml); pack bottle; used cost GBP 0.14

- Stonemill Ground Cumin - need 2 1/2 tsp (5g); pack jar; used cost GBP 0.10
- Stonemill Smoked Paprika - need 2 1/2 tsp (5g); pack jar; used cost GBP 0.10

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft poached eggs on hot buttered white sourdough, seasoned simply at the plate.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 25 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 15g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- 20 cm saucepan with lid
- Slotted spoon
- Oven grill or normal toaster route
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: set out 3 slices of white sourdough and 15g unsalted butter.
- Egg cups: set out 8 eggs; crack into cups if you want cleaner poaching.
- Finish station: keep the salt and pepper next to the plates.

## Method

### 1. Heat the poaching water

Bring the saucepan of water to a gentle simmer. Lower the heat so the water is moving but not bubbling hard.

### 2. Toast and butter the sourdough

Toast the sourdough until golden and firm. Butter each slice while it is hot so the butter melts into the toast.

Uses:

- White sourdough bread - 3 slices
- Unsalted butter - 15g

### 3. Poach the eggs

Slide the eggs into the water in controlled rounds if the pan is crowded. Cook gently until the whites are set and the yolks are still soft.

Uses:

- Eggs - 8

### 4. Plate and season

Lift the eggs out with a slotted spoon and drain briefly. Luke and Sarah get 3 eggs each. Louis gets 2 eggs. Season each plate with salt and black pepper.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Poached eggs - all

## Plate

### Luke

- Pantry: 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g.
- Dairy: 5g unsalted butter.
- Dairy: 3 poached eggs.

### Sarah

- Pantry: 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g.
- Dairy: 5g unsalted butter.
- Dairy: 3 poached eggs.

### Louis

- Pantry: 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g.
- Dairy: 5g unsalted butter.
- Dairy: 2 poached eggs.

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 377.2 kcal, 24.4g protein, 28.3g carbs, 19.3g fat, GBP 0.86, 95.1 fullness
- Sarah: 377.2 kcal, 24.4g protein, 28.3g carbs, 19.3g fat, GBP 0.86, 95.1 fullness
- Louis: 310.1 kcal, 17.9g protein, 28.3g carbs, 14.7g fat, GBP 0.67, 74.6 fullness

## Cook's Notes

- Butter the toast as soon as it comes out, then put the eggs on top.
- If the pan feels crowded, poach the eggs in two or three controlled rounds.
- Plate Sarah and Louis first if useful, then finish Luke's 3-egg plate.
- Season after the eggs land so the toast stays crisp and the yolk carries the salt and pepper.

# Buttered Poached Eggs On White Sourdough Toast - Plate Portions

Source: saved `datasheet.json` finished serving elements, person rows, or component shares. The renderer does not invent serving splits.

| Component | Luke | Sarah | Louis | Basis |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Buttered Sourdough Toast | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | 1 buttered slice | count_each_with_direct_amount |
| Poached Eggs | 3 eggs | 3 eggs | 2 eggs | count_each |

## Ingredient Rows

### Luke

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 1 slice (60g); 138 kcal; 5g protein; GBP 0.23; fullness 33.6

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Buttery Scrambled Eggs On White Sourdough Toast

Soft scrambled eggs cooked slowly in butter and spooned over hot buttered white sourdough.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 18 minutes

Yield: 3 plates

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Merevale British Free Range Mixed Weight Eggs - 8 eggs
- Cowbelle British Unsalted Butter - 23g total

### Pantry

- Specially Selected White Sourdough Sliced Loaf - 3 slices, about 180g total

## Equipment

- Saute pan
- Jug
- Fork or small whisk
- Silicone spatula
- Toaster
- Plates

## Mise En Place

- Toast board: set out 3 slices of white sourdough and 15g butter for the toast.
- Egg jug: crack 8 eggs into a jug, then add a light pinch of salt and a light grind of black pepper per person.
- Pan station: warm the saute pan on the lowest heat and keep 8g butter ready for the pan.

## Method

### 1. Warm the saute pan

Put the saute pan on the lowest heat for a few minutes so the base warms gently.

### 2. Whisk the eggs

Whisk the eggs in the jug until the yolks and whites are mostly combined.

Uses:

- Eggs - 8

### 3. Toast and butter the sourdough

Put the sourdough in the toaster. When the slices are done, butter each one while hot.

Uses:

- White sourdough bread - 3 slices
- Unsalted butter - 15g

### 4. Cook the eggs softly

Melt 8g butter in the warm pan. Pour in the eggs, then slowly scrape the bottom up with the spatula until the eggs are almost cooked but still soft.

Uses:

- Whisked eggs - all
- Unsalted butter - 8g

### 5. Finish off the heat

Take the pan off the heat while the eggs are still slightly glossy. Finish the toast, then spoon the scrambled eggs over the buttered slices.

Uses:

- Buttered sourdough toast - all
- Scrambled eggs - all

## Plate

### Luke

- Pantry: 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g.
- Dairy: 8g unsalted butter total.
- Dairy: 3 eggs' worth of soft scrambled egg.

### Sarah

- Pantry: 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g.
- Dairy: 8g unsalted butter total.
- Dairy: 3 eggs' worth of soft scrambled egg.

### Louis

- Pantry: 1 slice white sourdough toast, about 60g.
- Dairy: 7g unsalted butter total.
- Dairy: 2 eggs' worth of soft scrambled egg.

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 399.5 kcal, 24.4g protein, 28.3g carbs, 21.8g fat, GBP 0.88, 95.1 fullness
- Sarah: 399.5 kcal, 24.4g protein, 28.3g carbs, 21.8g fat, GBP 0.88, 95.1 fullness
- Louis: 325.0 kcal, 17.9g protein, 28.3g carbs, 16.4g fat, GBP 0.69, 74.6 fullness

## Cook's Notes

- Keep the heat low. The eggs should cook gently rather than sizzle.
- Scrape the bottom of the pan slowly so the eggs form soft curds.
- Take the pan off the heat before the eggs look fully cooked; the carry-over heat finishes them.
- The butter total is measured as 15g for the toast and 8g for the pan.

# Buttery Scrambled Eggs On White Sourdough Toast - Plate Portions

Source: saved `datasheet.json` finished serving elements, person rows, or component shares. The renderer does not invent serving splits.

| Component | Luke | Sarah | Louis | Basis |

recipes.recipe_card_markdown:
# Strawberry Banana Fat-Free Greek Yoghurt Bowl With Honey And Chia

Fresh strawberry and banana bowls with fat-free Greek-style yoghurt, honey and chia.

Serves: Luke, Sarah and Louis

Time: 8 minutes

Yield: 3 bowls

Meal role: breakfast

## Ingredients

### Dairy

- Brooklea Fat Free Greek Style Yogurt - 750g total

### Fruit/Veg

- Nature's Pick Bananas - 2 bananas total
- Nature's Pick Strawberries - 140g total

### Pantry

- Everyday Essentials Clear Honey - 15g total, about 3/4 tbsp
- Chia seeds - 14g total, split as 5g Luke, 5g Sarah and 4g Louis

## Equipment

- 3 breakfast bowls
- Chopping board
- Knife
- Scales
- Spoon

## Mise En Place

- Fruit board: wash and hull the strawberries, then slice the strawberries and bananas.
- Bowl set: set out the 3 bowls, scales, spoon, yoghurt, honey and chia seeds.

## Method

### 1. Prep the fruit

Wash and hull the strawberries. Slice the strawberries and bananas into bite-sized pieces.

### 2. Spoon in the yoghurt

Spoon the yoghurt into the bowls: Luke 300g, Sarah 270g and Louis 180g.

### 3. Add the fruit

Share the 2 sliced bananas across the bowls: larger shares for Luke and Sarah, and a smaller child share for Louis. Add 50g strawberries for Luke and Sarah, and 40g strawberries for Louis.

### 4. Finish with honey and chia

Add the honey and chia, then serve straight away.

## Plate

### Luke

- Dairy: 300g fat-free Greek-style yoghurt.
- Fruit/Veg: larger share of the sliced banana and 50g strawberries.
- Pantry: 5g honey and 5g chia seeds.

### Sarah

- Dairy: 270g fat-free Greek-style yoghurt.
- Fruit/Veg: larger share of the sliced banana and 50g strawberries.
- Pantry: 4g honey and 5g chia seeds.

### Louis

- Dairy: 180g fat-free Greek-style yoghurt.
- Fruit/Veg: smaller child share of the sliced banana and 40g strawberries.
- Pantry: 6g honey and 4g chia seeds.

## Nutrition And Cost

- Luke: 300.5 kcal, 19.7g protein, 51.5g carbs, 2.5g fat, GBP 0.98, 110.0 fullness
- Sarah: 279.0 kcal, 17.9g protein, 48.1g carbs, 2.4g fat, GBP 0.93, 100.5 fullness
- Louis: 200.8 kcal, 12.1g protein, 35.3g carbs, 1.9g fat, GBP 0.69, 71.0 fullness

## Cook's Notes

- Serve straight away so the banana stays fresh and the chia does not thicken the yoghurt too much.
- This is a lighter adult breakfast; pair with a protein-led lunch later if needed.
- Louis gets the smaller banana share.
- Louis is cleared for yoghurt in this bowl.
